generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor providing 3D environment mapping and object detection for ACC applications. Processes raw point cloud data into clustered object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance DepthCameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera for stereo vision and proximity sensing, integrated with LiDAR data for enhanced spatial awareness."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Telemetry system collecting real-time kinematic states (velocity, acceleration, orientation) from motor, steering, and servo actuation systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance ActuationFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Feedback system monitoring motor and servo actuation responses (torque, angular position) for closed-loop control validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationFeedbackData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw and processed LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from LiDARPointCloudSensor, including clustered object tracks and environmental maps."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Stereo vision and depth data from DepthCameraSensor for proximity sensing and object detection augmentation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DepthCameraSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry data including velocity, acceleration, and orientation from VehicleStateSensor."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor and servo actuation feedback data (torque, angular position) for control system validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ActuationFeedbackSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component processing raw LiDAR data into structured object tracks and environmental maps using clustering algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateAggregationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model aggregating motor, steering, and servo telemetry into unified vehicle state representation with unit standardization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured LiDAR data after clustering and tracking, including object tracks and spatial maps for ACC visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance DepthCameraDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed depth camera data integrated with LiDAR tracks for enhanced environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance UnifiedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Standardized vehicle state data combining kinematic states (velocity, acceleration) with temporal synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ActuationFeedbackDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed actuation feedback data validated against control commands for system integrity."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlValidationEnabler
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed driving path projection based on vehicle state and object tracks for ACC path planning."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Detected and tracked objects in proximity (e.g., lead vehicles, obstacles) for collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalDataLogs : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Collected and processed data saved for post-analysis, including vehicle state, object tracks, and control actions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData UnifiedVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReportingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based software component implementing point cloud clustering and tracking algorithms for LiDAR data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data translation component standardizing message formats, aligning temporal synchronization, and performing unit conversions."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware integrating LiDAR and depth camera data streams for enhanced environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization component rendering real-time data (vehicle state, object tracks, projected paths) for operator monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Control logic implementing lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and actuation command generation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ControlValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Validation component comparing actuation feedback with control commands to ensure system integrity."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ReportingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Report generation component creating structured logs (CSV/JSON) from historical data for post-analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 framework enabling modular inter-process communication between functional entities via topics and services."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance UDPCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi UDP transport layer ensuring low-latency communication between Physical Twin and Digital Twin entities."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonEdgeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer for on-board edge AI processing of sensor data and control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment for control algorithm prototyping and co-simulation with ROS2."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data (projected driving path, detected object tracks) with data collection, saving, and report generation."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, ObjectDetectionInsight, HistoricalDataInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC computation from on-board hardware to external cloud/edge environment, performing lead vehicle detection and desired speed computation."
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetectionInsight, DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight, ActuationCommandInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Geographically distributed operation of ACC with remote enabling/disabling, speed setting, and emergency braking capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlInsight, EmergencyBrakingInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of vehicle kinematic states (velocity, acceleration, orientation) for ACC operation."
]

instance ObjectDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection and tracking of objects in proximity (lead vehicles, obstacles) for collision avoidance."
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Access to saved vehicle state and object track data for post-analysis and troubleshooting."
]

instance LeadVehicleDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of lead vehicles and their relative positions for adaptive speed regulation."
]

instance DesiredSpeedCalculationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computation of optimal desired speed based on inter-vehicle distance and safety margins."
]

instance ActuationCommandInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Generated control commands (motor voltage, steering angles) for vehicle actuation."
]

instance RemoteControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Remote monitoring and control capabilities for geographically distributed ACC operation."
]

instance EmergencyBrakingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of critical scenarios requiring immediate emergency braking intervention."
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote activation of Adaptive Cruise Control functionality."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote deactivation of Adaptive Cruise Control functionality."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote adjustment of desired cruise speed via user interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance IssueEmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic emergency braking command issued in critical collision scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance ElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller managing motor voltage commands for speed regulation in the physical twin."
]

instance LowerLevelChassisControlSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Control system interfacing with steering and servo mechanisms for vehicle actuation based on high-level commands."
]

// C1: Physical Twin Components
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle with LiDAR, depth cameras, and actuation systems for ACC testing."
    base:contains LiDARPointCloudSensor, DepthCameraSensor, VehicleStateSensor, ActuationFeedbackSensor,
                ElectronicSpeedController, LowerLevelChassisControlSystem
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the physical twin providing computational models and data management for ACC."
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringModel, VehicleStateAggregationModel, ControlEnabler, VisualizationEnabler,
                ReportingEnabler, ROS2MiddlewareEnabler, UDPCommunicationEnabler, NVIDIAJetsonEdgeEnabler
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interface component for monitoring and controlling the ACC system."
    base:contains VisualizationEnabler, DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance Environment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS is governed by the following control flows:
  1. **Command Types**:
     - **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) commands**: Desired speed/acceleration (computed by **Controlling FE**).
     - **Motor actuation commands**: Voltage/current signals (generated by **Actuation FE**).
     - **Steering servo commands**: Angular position adjustments (derived from ACC path planning).
  2. **Control Message Formats**:
     - **ACC Output**: Structured messages containing lead vehicle distance, relative speed, and projected path.
     - **Actuation Commands**: Standardized voltage/current signals (aligned via **Data Translation FE**).
  3. **Validation & Authentication**:
     - **Data Translation FE** ensures message integrity through serialization and unit validation.
     - **Controlling FE** cross-references sensor data (e.g., LiDAR tracks) before issuing commands.
  4. **Feedback Systems**:
     - **Motor/Steering Servo Feedback**: Closed-loop via **Actuation FE**, confirming command execution (e.g., actual voltage applied).
     - **Vehicle State Updates**: Returned to **Controlling FE** for real-time adjustments (e.g., speed deviations).
  5. **Conflict Resolution**:
     - **Priority-based arbitration** (unspecified rules, but implied by ACC’s dominance over manual overrides).
     - **Data Translation FE** resolves message format conflicts via alignment protocols.
  6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
     - **Actuation FE** acknowledges command receipt/execution (e.g., motor servo confirmation signals).
     - **ROS2 QoS Policies** (implied but not explicitly detailed) may enforce reliability for critical commands.
  **Note**: The system offloads ACC computation to the Digital Twin (hybrid deployment), while actuation remains on the physical entity."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter clock synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards. The system does not explicitly specify varying time scales for different services or latency requirements beyond this synchronization constraint, but the 100 ms threshold implies real-time or near-real-time operational constraints for data exchange and processing between the Physical Twin Entity (sensors/actuators), Digital Twin Entity (data management/control), and User Entity (visualization/control)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates coordination through an **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)-inspired entity-based design**, where software components are mapped to **Functional Entities (FEs)** and deployed as **ROS2 packages** on distributed hardware. Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  1. **Entity-Based Decomposition**: The system is partitioned into three primary entities—*Physical Twin*, *Digital Twin*, and *User*—each with distinct responsibilities (e.g., sensor/actuator interfacing, data control, user interaction).
  2. **ROS2 Middleware**: Enables **inter-component communication** via topics/services, facilitating real-time data exchange (e.g., between *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* in the Physical Twin Entity and visualization in the User Entity).
  3. **Resource Management**: The Physical Twin Entity abstracts hardware resources (compute, sensors, actuators) to host both general and specific automotive functionalities, requiring careful ownership and engineering process alignment.
  4. **Deviations from ISO 23247**: The Physical Twin Entity’s expanded scope (beyond resource-specific OME) and the splitting of the *User Interface FE* into subcomponents (*3D Visualization* and *Control Panel*) introduce architectural complexity, necessitating explicit coordination during system integration.
  The constellation lacks centralized orchestration but relies on **modular, entity-specific ROS2 packages** for dynamic resource allocation and fault isolation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard (Part 2) does not explicitly detail **fidelity requirements** (e.g., model resolution, temporal accuracy) or **validation/verification methods** for the Digital Twin Entity (DTE). However, the framework implies:
  - **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)** and its digital representation (e.g., sensor noise, model simplifications).
  - **Error detection/correction** could be handled by **Functional Entities (FEs)** (e.g., via real-time data reconciliation between OME and DTE), though no specific mechanisms (e.g., Kalman filters, machine learning) are prescribed.
  - **Quality assurance** is decentralized across entities, with the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** responsible for ensuring data integrity from OMEs. The standard lacks explicit guidelines for **uncertainty quantification** (e.g., probabilistic bounds) or **verification protocols** (e.g., traceability matrices). A public demonstration of the DT ([2]) may provide empirical insights, but no technical details are included in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is defined as follows:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **ROS2 Network**: Used for **intra-entity communication** between software components (e.g., *Data Pre-Processing FE*, *Controlling FE*, *Actuation FE*), enabling modular, publish-subscribe-based data exchange.
   - **Wi-Fi (UDP)**: Employed for **inter-entity communication** (e.g., between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity), ensuring low-latency transmission of sensor and control data.

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical**: Sensor data (e.g., LiDAR, motor/servo telemetry) flows from the **Physical Twin Entity** to the **Digital Twin Entity** via Wi-Fi/UDP, while processed data and commands circulate internally via ROS2.
   - **Decentralized Components**: Software components communicate asynchronously, with no single point of failure for critical data paths.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **High Bandwidth**: Required for LiDAR point cloud data (high-resolution spatial data) and real-time vehicle state updates.
   - **Low Latency**: Critical for control loops (e.g., ACC commands) and visualization updates, with Wi-Fi/UDP prioritized for time-sensitive data.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **ROS2 Reliability Policies**: Configurable QoS (Quality of Service) policies for ROS2 topics to ensure message delivery (e.g., best-effort or reliable queues).
   - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a fail-safe for message serialization and unit conversion, reducing protocol mismatches.
   - **Not Explicitly Redundant**: No mention of hardware/software redundancy (e.g., backup nodes or failover paths) in the document.

5. **Security Protocols for Network Communication**:
   - Not explicitly detailed in the document. Implied security measures may include:
     - **ROS2 Authentication**: Potential use of ROS2’s built-in security plugins (e.g., TLS for encrypted communication).
     - **Wi-Fi Encryption**: Standard Wi-Fi security (e.g., WPA3) for inter-entity communication, though not specified.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s Hosting & Deployment** infrastructure is characterized by:
  1. **Deployment Models**:
     - **Hybrid (Edge + Cloud)**: ACC computation offloaded to the Digital Twin (cloud/edge), while actuation remains on the **Physical Twin (NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano)**.
     - **Computation Offloading**: Only the ACC system is migrated to the Digital Twin for centralized processing.
  2. **Hosting Platforms**:
     - **Physical Twin Entity**: On-premise (vehicle-mounted **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano**).
     - **Digital Twin Entity**: Cloud/edge (unspecified provider, but implied by Wi-Fi offloading).
  3. **Computational & Storage Requirements**:
     - **ACC Controlling FE**: Requires real-time processing (e.g., path planning, object tracking).
     - **Data Storage FE**: Archives reports and processed data (unspecified capacity).
  4. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
     - **ROS2’s modular design** allows dynamic addition of nodes (e.g., new sensors).
     - **Wi-Fi bandwidth** may limit scalability for high-density deployments (unspecified thresholds).
  5. **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
     - **Data Translation FE** ensures consistent message formats across entities.
     - **No explicit redundancy** (e.g., backup nodes, failover servers) is documented.
     - **ROS2 QoS Policies** (implied) may enforce reliability for critical services (e.g., ACC).
  **Note**: The system prioritizes real-time performance (≤100 ms latency) over high availability."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines an **entity-based framework** for manufacturing Digital Twins. Key compliance aspects include:
  - **Entity-based Reference Architecture (RA)**: Mandates decomposition into **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**, each with defined responsibilities.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: While not explicitly named, the architecture implies compatibility with **industrial communication protocols** (e.g., **OPC Unified Architecture**) for data exchange between entities.
  - **Certification Requirements**: Not specified in the document, but adherence to ISO 23247 ensures alignment with **manufacturing-specific digital twin standards**.
  - **Compliance Frameworks**: The standard provides a **structured governance model** for Digital Twin development, ensuring traceability and consistency across lifecycle phases. **Information exchange** (Part 4 of ISO 23247) is referenced but not detailed in the provided context."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents reference **ISO 15622:2018** (Intelligent Transport Systems - Adaptive Cruise Control Systems) and **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 Release 15** (V2X services), which implicitly address **safety-critical system considerations** through performance requirements and test procedures for adaptive cruise control (ACC) systems. Specifically, **ISO 15622:2018** defines **functional safety requirements** for ACC systems, including **fail-safe mechanisms** and **risk assessment approaches** to ensure safe operation under dynamic traffic conditions. The **3GPP TS 22.185** standard outlines **cybersecurity protocols** for V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) communication, emphasizing **authentication systems** and **threat mitigation strategies** to protect against spoofing, replay attacks, and unauthorized access in cooperative driving scenarios. However, explicit details on **access control mechanisms**, **data anonymization**, or **compliance with data protection regulations** are not provided in the given documents."
]
